content.title: MAGE-RAG: Multigranular Adaptive Graph Evidence for Agentic Multimodal RAG in Long-Document QA
content.summary: MAGE-RAG introduces a multigranular adaptive graph evidence framework that enables agentic AI systems to perform accurate multimodal retrieval-augmented generation over long documents, addressing key limitations in existing RAG pipelines that struggle with cross-modal reasoning across large corpora.
content.summary_1: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems for long documents face fundamental challenges when content spans multiple modalities (text, tables, figures) and requires multi-hop reasoning. MAGE-RAG (Multigranular Adaptive Graph Evidence for Agentic Multimodal RAG) proposes a graph-based evidence framework that operates at multiple granularity levels to capture both fine-grained and document-level relationships. The system builds adaptive evidence graphs that connect text, visual, and structured data, enabling agentic traversal for complex question answering tasks. By treating long-document QA as a graph retrieval and reasoning problem rather than a simple dense retrieval problem, MAGE-RAG addresses the cross-modal alignment challenge that degrades conventional RAG pipelines on complex real-world documents. The approach spans Information Retrieval (cs.IR), Computation and Language (cs.CL), Databases, and Multimedia domains.
content.body_text: MAGE-RAG: Multigranular Adaptive Graph Evidence for Agentic Multimodal RAG in Long-Document QA addresses the challenge of retrieving and reasoning over long documents containing multiple modalities. The system introduces a multigranular graph evidence framework that captures relationships at different levels of document granularity—from individual sentences and tables to full sections—enabling adaptive retrieval strategies for complex multi-hop questions. The agentic component allows dynamic evidence path traversal across the graph, supporting reasoning patterns that require connecting disparate pieces of information spread across long documents. Authors: Yilong Zuo, Xunkai Li, Jing Yuan, Qiangqiang Dai, Hongchao Qin, Ronghua Li. Subjects: Information Retrieval (cs.IR); Artificial Intelligence (cs.AI); Computation and Language (cs.CL); Databases (cs.DB); Multimedia (cs.MM).

tech_insights.impact_summary: MAGE-RAG advances the state of enterprise RAG systems for document-heavy workflows (legal, medical, financial) by combining graph-based multi-hop reasoning with multimodal content handling, directly enabling more accurate AI assistants over complex document repositories.
actions_developer: Explore MAGE-RAG's multigranular graph architecture for enterprise document QA systems requiring cross-modal reasoning
Evaluate adaptive graph evidence retrieval against dense vector search for long-document RAG benchmarks
actions_business: Assess MAGE-RAG for knowledge management and document intelligence products handling mixed-modality enterprise content
Consider graph-based RAG as an upgrade path for existing RAG pipelines struggling with long legal/financial documents
